serviceNotes.engine oil: BMW Condition Based Service (CBS) sets engine oil and filter replacement from the service display based on driving conditions, cold starts, and fuel quality—not a single fixed km figure. A 30,000 km mfr row models the typical maximum distance before the display calls for an oil service on many EU models; follow the vehicle display and annual minimums. Pure-electric i7 has no engine oil—ignore this row for BEV.
serviceNotes.cabin filter: Cabin (micro) filter replacement is driven by the service display on CBS vehicles; 30,000 km is a typical planning interval when aligning with major services.
serviceNotes.air filter: garage.wiki BMW maintenance tables tie engine air filter, fuel filter (diesel), and spark plugs (petrol) to "with each third engine oil change". At a ~30,000 km CBS oil cadence this implies roughly 90,000 km; this platform uses 90,000 km as the mfr-aligned distance. Confirm with your service display and owner's literature. N/A for i7 BEV.
serviceNotes.brake fluid: BMW specifies brake fluid replacement every 2 years (first change at 3 years on some models per BMW maintenance SIBs). Distance equivalent uses ~30,000 km/year as a planning proxy; always respect the time limit. See BMW X5 service documentation summary at bmwx5info.com (brake fluid change interval every 2 years).
serviceNotes.check brake pads: Brake pad thickness is monitored via CBS wear sensors and inspection at scheduled services; 30,000 km is a reasonable inspection cadence between major services.
serviceNotes.spark plugs: Petrol engines only; N/A for i7 BEV. garage.wiki BMW maintenance tables tie spark plugs to "with each third engine oil change" (~90,000 km at typical CBS oil cadence).
serviceNotes.fuel filter: Diesel engines only; N/A for petrol and i7 BEV.
serviceNotes.gearbox fluid: Automatic and manual gear oil is often labelled lifetime; many specialists recommend exchange around 100,000 km for long-term health.
serviceNotes.coolant flush: Coolant inhibitor effectiveness declines with age; preventive full exchange around 120,000 km is a common workshop recommendation. i7 also has HV thermal coolant—follow BMW EV service data.
serviceNotes.intake carbon clean: Direct-injection turbo engines can accumulate intake valve deposits; walnut or chemical cleaning around 120,000 km restores airflow. N/A for i7 BEV.
